features.summary: AMD delivered strong Q1 2025 results with revenue of $7.4 billion, up 36% year-over-year, driven by 57% growth in data center and 28% growth in client/gaming segments. Data center revenue reached $3.7 billion with continued EPYC CPU share gains and strong Instinct GPU adoption. Client revenue grew 68% year-over-year to $2.3 billion, driven by higher ASPs from premium Ryzen processors and strong desktop performance. The company completed the ZT Systems acquisition to enhance rack-scale AI solutions capabilities and remains on track to launch MI350 series GPUs mid-year.
features.management_outlook: Management expects strong double-digit revenue growth for 2025 despite headwinds from new China export controls on MI308 GPUs, which will reduce revenue by approximately $1.5 billion for the full year. The company anticipates continued share gains with Zen 5 EPYC and Ryzen CPUs, ramping MI350 series production in the second half, and return to growth in embedded and semi-custom businesses. Q2 guidance is $7.4 billion ±$300 million, representing 27% year-over-year growth despite the $700 million China-related headwind.
features.risks: New export license requirements for MI308 shipments to China create a $1.5 billion revenue headwind for 2025, with $700 million impact in Q2 alone. Ongoing macro and trade policy uncertainties, including potential AI diffusion rules implementation, pose additional regulatory risks. The company faces execution challenges in scaling rack-level AI solutions and managing the integration of ZT Systems while competing against established players in the AI accelerator market.
features.key_drivers: Strong adoption of fifth-generation EPYC Turin processors driving server CPU share gains across cloud and enterprise customers. Robust client business performance driven by premium Ryzen processor mix and strong desktop gaming demand. Successful Radeon 9070 series launch with record first-week sellout. Growing Instinct GPU deployments with major cloud providers and enterprise customers, supported by accelerated ROCm software development and expanding model compatibility including day-zero support for latest AI models.
